Asthma.reason: Beta-blockers cause bronchoconstriction and can trigger severe asthma attacks
Asthma.action: Avoid all beta-blockers in asthma. Use Amlodipine or ACE inhibitor for hypertension instead.
Avoid. If a beta-blocker is essential, use Bisoprolol with extreme caution and close monitoring.

Asthma.reason: Even cardioselective beta-blockers can worsen asthma at higher doses
Asthma.action: Use only if benefit outweighs risk. Start at lowest dose. Monitor breathing carefully.

Asthma.reason: Non-selective beta-blocker — highly risky in asthma
Aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease (AERD) in ~10% of asthmatics — triggers attacks
Asthma.action: Perform Aspirin challenge test first. If sensitive, avoid Aspirin and all NSAIDs.

Asthma.reason: NSAIDs can worsen asthma via COX inhibition
Asthma.action: Try Paracetamol instead. Avoid NSAIDs if Aspirin sensitivity is present.

CKD.reason: Metformin is renally cleared and accumulates in CKD causing fatal lactic acidosis
CKD.action: Stop Metformin if eGFR < 30. Reduce dose if eGFR 30-45. Monitor kidney function every 3 months.

CKD.reason: NSAIDs reduce renal blood flow via prostaglandin inhibition, rapidly accelerating CKD
CKD.action: Strictly avoid all NSAIDs in CKD. Use Paracetamol (max 2g/day) for pain.

CKD.reason: NSAID — same mechanism as Ibuprofen, acute kidney injury risk
NSAID nephrotoxicity causing acute kidney injury on CKD background
CKD.action: Avoid. Use Paracetamol for pain management.

CKD.reason: Allopurinol and its metabolite oxipurinol accumulate in CKD, increasing toxicity risk
CKD.action: Reduce Allopurinol dose based on eGFR. Consider Febuxostat as alternative.

CKD.reason: Potassium retention worsened in CKD — risk of dangerous hyperkalemia
CKD.action: Monitor potassium weekly. Avoid if potassium > 5.0 mEq/L.

CKD.reason: Thiazides are largely ineffective when eGFR < 30 and may worsen kidney function
CKD.action: Switch to loop diuretic (Furosemide) for fluid management in advanced CKD.

Diabetes.reason: Corticosteroids cause severe insulin resistance and dramatic blood sugar spikes
Diabetes.action: Monitor glucose 4x daily. Diabetes medications may need to be substantially increased.

Diabetes.reason: Thiazide diuretics impair glucose tolerance and worsen diabetes control
Diabetes.action: Monitor blood glucose more frequently. Consider switching to Furosemide or Amlodipine.

Diabetes.reason: High-dose Furosemide can mildly impair glucose metabolism

Hypertension.reason: NSAIDs cause sodium and water retention, directly raising blood pressure by 3-5 mmHg on average
Hypertension.action: Avoid regular NSAID use. Use Paracetamol for pain. Monitor BP.

Hypertension.reason: NSAID — same sodium-retaining mechanism, can cause treatment-resistant hypertension

CAD.reason: Diclofenac increases cardiovascular events — banned in many countries for CAD patients
CAD.action: Avoid in CAD. Use Paracetamol for pain. If NSAID needed, Naproxen has better cardiac safety.

CAD.reason: COX-2 inhibitors increase cardiac event risk
CAD.action: Avoid if possible. If necessary, use lowest dose for shortest time.

Gout.reason: Thiazides reduce uric acid excretion, triggering gout attacks
Gout.action: Switch antihypertensive to Losartan (which has mild uricosuric activity) or Amlodipine.

Gout.reason: Low-dose Aspirin competes with uric acid excretion, raising uric acid levels
Gout.action: Continue Aspirin if needed for cardiac protection but increase Allopurinol dose accordingly.

Gout.reason: Loop diuretics raise uric acid levels by increasing reabsorption
Gout.action: Monitor uric acid levels. May need to increase Allopurinol dose.

GERD.reason: NSAIDs damage gastric mucosa and worsen acid reflux significantly
GERD.action: Avoid NSAIDs. Use Paracetamol. Always take with full PPI cover if NSAID is unavoidable.

GERD.reason: Aspirin irritates the stomach lining, worsening GERD
GERD.action: Use enteric-coated Aspirin. Take with food. Add Pantoprazole prophylactically.
Always co-prescribe a PPI when using corticosteroids in GERD patients.

Liver Disease.reason: Paracetamol is metabolized in the liver. Even normal doses cause liver damage in liver disease
Liver Disease.action: Reduce dose to max 1.5g/day. Avoid alcohol completely. Consider alternatives.

Liver Disease.reason: Statins are metabolized in the liver and can worsen liver function
Liver Disease.action: Check liver enzymes before starting. Avoid if ALT > 3x normal.

Liver Disease.reason: Metronidazole is hepatically metabolized and accumulates in liver failure
Liver Disease.action: Reduce dose and frequency. Monitor liver function tests.

Heart Failure.reason: NSAIDs cause sodium retention and vasoconstriction, severely worsening heart failure
Heart Failure.action: Strictly contraindicated in heart failure. Use Paracetamol for all pain management.

Heart Failure.reason: NSAIDs worsen heart failure and increase hospitalization risk 2x
Heart Failure.action: Avoid. The risk of acute decompensation is very high.

Heart Failure.reason: Calcium channel blocker with negative inotropic effect worsens heart failure
Heart Failure.action: Contraindicated. Use Amlodipine if a CCB is needed.
